items.title: As main street shops vanish, could they help solve housing shortages?

children.content: As businesses shut up shop in regional main streets, the government is weighing up whether they could help solve the housing crisis.

gallery.altText: A for-lease sign on the left in an image showing the footpath of  regional shopping street.

thumbnail.altText: A for-lease sign on the left in an image showing the footpath of  regional shopping street.

items.title: Accused killer denies murder but admits burning woman's body, court told

children.content: Johnathan Paul Parker allegedly told a friend that he had strangled his housemate, Krystal Marshall, and asked for help to dispose of her body, a court has heard.

image.altText: A woman with sunglasses on her head.

items.title: More rain to follow overnight storm and 50k lightning strikes in parts of SA

children.content: Wet and wild weather is lashing parts of South Australia with the Bureau of Meteorology warning about wind risk during storms.

image.altText: People cross a pedestrian crossing while carrying umbrellas with a wet ground

items.title: Volunteers say National Trust of SA warned them against speaking publicly

children.content: Volunteers who spoke to the media say they felt "threatened" with formal warnings from the National Trust of South Australia. They are now preparing to share their experiences in a parliamentary inquiry.

image.altText: A woman with cropped white hair and a necklace in a living room

items.title: Woman 'still here to see grandkids grow' welcomes free HIV treatment in SA

children.content: When Kath Leane was diagnosed with HIV almost 40 years ago, she feared she would never see her children — let alone her grandchildren — grow up. But the antiretroviral treatment which helped make that possible is today being made free for all South Australians.

image.altText: A grandmother with long blond hair sits in an armchair in her home with a bookcase behind her

items.title: E-motorcyclist filmed doing wheelie, crashing while police attend another collision

children.content: SA Police publishes a video of a teenager on an electric motorcycle doing a wheelie and then crashing into a car while they were attending another collision at the same location in Adelaide's southern suburbs.

image.altText: A motorcycle rider doing a wheelie past commercial buildings

items.title: Patrons farewell historical pub in outback town amid spate of closures

children.content: There used to be more than 70 pubs in Broken Hill, but now only a handful remain. The Australian Hotels Association warns hotels in remote locations need to diversify their business model in order to survive a rapidly changing hospitality landscape.

image.altText: An older man standing outside a country pub with a grimace on his face, as it rains in the background

items.title: Matthew Flinders's steamy letters reveal a hidden side to the man who named  Australia

children.content: Flinders circumnavigated and named Australia. Impressive stuff, but he also wrote many impassioned letters and lived a life full of crushes.

gallery.altText: A letter from 1800, cursive ink writing and faded paper

thumbnail.altText: A letter from 1800, cursive ink writing and faded paper

Senegal players crowd the ref to complain about a penalty decision

articles.title: When do the Socceroos play next and how to watch the knockout match

children.content: Australia's journey at the 2026 World Cup continues this weekend as the Socceroos take on Egypt. Here's everything you need to know.

image.altText: Players line up and sing their national anthem.

children.content: Facing World Cup elimination at the hands of DR Congo, England needed a hero to step up when it mattered most. Harry Kane was that man.

image.altText: Harry Kane from behind holds out his hands in front of a crowd

children.content: Belgium converts a penalty in extra-time to beat Senegal 3-2, while Harry Kane strikes twice after half-time to defeat DR Congo 2-1. Follow live.

updates.title: Team news: USA v Bosnia and Herzegovina
FT: Belgium through with latest winner in World Cup history

items.title: 'One last crack' to restart Whyalla’s blast furnace as workers face nervous wait

children.content: The aging blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks could be permanently shut down before the steelworks transitions to a direct-reduced iron plant and electric arc furnace, placing hundreds of jobs at risk.

image.altText: A man wearing an orange jacket and white hard hat stands outside a large industrial building

items.title: 'No genuine opportunity' to prevent crash that killed two women, coroner finds

children.content: A South Australian coroner says it is an "unfortunate truth" that there was no way for police to predict or prevent a collision that killed a senior police officer and another woman in 2020.

image.altText: An image of Joanne Shanahan and Tania McNeill.

children.content: The federal government has backed down on its aged care assessment tool to determine how much funding older Australians get. Follow live.

Labor MP welcomes gambling ad bill but calls for ban on inducements
Wells accuses Coalition of giving social media companies eight weeks to 'shred documents'

items.title: Influencers to be targeted in crackdown on gambling advertising

children.content: The Albanese government will target social media influencers and individuals who promote gambling on social media as part of its proposed laws restricting advertising.

image.altText: A woman with the parliament house flag in the background.

items.title: Thousands of survivors of child sexual abuse face looming redress backlog

children.content: At its current processing rate, the scheme will not be able to clear its backlog before it closes.

image.altText: Chris Coombes

items.title: Before and after images show widespread impact of Venezuelan quakes

children.content: Venezuela's devastating earthquakes flattened buildings and coated parts of the northern coast in dust and debris, satellite imagery shows.

gallery.altText: An aerial image of rows of residential homes and an ocean-side boat marina with significant earthquake damage.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial image of rows of residential homes and an ocean-side boat marina with significant earthquake damage.

items.title: NSW sergeant 'snuck a look' for ex-cop mate accused of domestic violence

children.content: A NSW Police officer has admitted to unlawfully accessing the force's internal database to locate details about a domestic violence complaint against his friend.

image.altText: Photo illustration with police officer silhouette and text messages from story

items.title: US Navy crew member missing as chopper goes down in Arabian Sea

children.content: A US Navy service member is missing and three others are injured after their MH-60S Sea Hawk helicopter went down in the Arabian Sea.

image.altText: A US Navy grey fighter helicopter hovering above the deck of an aircraft carrier, where three people in jackets are standing.

items.title: Russian daredevil couple scale Empire State Building in proposal stunt

children.content: Two Russian daredevils, Angela Nikolau and Vanya Beerkus, claim to be the duo who scaled the 102-storey, 443-metre tower for an apparent show of love and a marriage proposal.

gallery.altText: Two people in black clothing and masks standing atop a skyscraper antenna with a black protest banner in the wind.

thumbnail.altText: Two people in black clothing and masks standing atop a skyscraper antenna with a black protest banner in the wind.

items.title: 'My last Wimbledon': Kyrgios bows out with typically erratic doubles outing

children.content: Australian Nick Kyrgios says he has almost certainly played his final Wimbledon match after losing in the first round of the men's doubles alongside Alexander Bublik.

image.altText: Nick Kyrgios runs forward during a doubles match at Wimbledon. Alexander Bublik is behind him.

items.title: Nobody knows where John Frum first came from, but he is god in this village

children.content: On Vanuatu's Tanna Island, I immerse myself in a culture that worships a shape-shifting spirit known as John Frum.

image.altText: Kate dances with a woman in a field in Vanuatu.

items.title: Jackass began with a gun shot; now it's a $500m franchise

children.content: For more than 25 years, prank franchise Jackass has been delighting teens and terrifying parents. Now the gang is heading to the cinemas for what could be their last ride.

items.title: Extremists using 'codes' to evade detection online, royal commission hears

children.content: Far-right individuals adopt memes as a way to push "more palatable" antisemitism, which is less likely to be picked up by social media moderators.

items.title: Spider venom spray could save Australia's honey bees from varroa mites

children.content: Queensland researchers are testing novel ways to kill varroa mites, which have developed a resistance to standard treatments.

items.title: The US and UK have banned kids heading the ball. Should Australia follow?

children.content: With all eyes on the World Cup, Australian football's management of the risks associated with the practice of heading the ball, such as dementia and CTE, is in the spotlight.

items.title: Pat Conroy to announce defence shake-up aimed at cost blowouts

items.title: Parents say latest asbestos find in WA school grounds is one of many
